content.problemStatement: Write a program for the coding challenge "Minimal Square". Refer to LeetCode description: https://codeforces.com/problemset/problem/1360/A
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. Then T test cases follow. The first line of each test case contains a single integer N. The second line contains N space-separated integers.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, print the side length of the smallest square that can contain all the given points.

hidden.label: tricky case / empty-like / negative if allowed

solution.approach: Calculate the maximum x and y coordinates, then return the maximum of these two values as the side length of the square.

code.Python3:
import sys
T = int(input())
for _ in range(T):
    N = int(input())
    max_x = max_y = 0
    for _ in range(N):
        x, y = map(int, input().split())
        max_x = max(max_x, x)
        max_y = max(max_y, y)
    print(max(max_x, max_y))
